entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The crop species being evaluated for disease resistance.

entities.name: DRY ROOT ROT
entities.description: A disease affecting the roots of chickpea plants.

entities.name: R. BATATICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes dry root rot.

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: The scientific name for the pathogen causing dry root rot.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The international research center where breeding lines were identified.

entities.name: WILT
entities.description: A disease mentioned as part of the field resistance trials.

entities.name: COLLAR ROT
entities.description: A disease affecting the collar region of the plant.

entities.name: SCLEROTIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes collar rot.

entities.name: CORTICIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: The scientific name for the pathogen causing collar rot.

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of intentionally introducing the pathogen to seedlings.

entities.name: DISEASE SCORE
entities.description: A 1-9 rating scale used to categorize the level of resistance or susceptibility.

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO DRY ROOT ROT
entities.description: The ability of a chickpea line to withstand dry root rot infection.

entities.name: ICC 14395
entities.description: A chickpea germplasm accession found to be resistant to dry root rot.

entities.name: ICCV 2
entities.description: A chickpea cultivar found to be resistant to dry root rot.

entities.name: ICCX830203-BH-BH-11H
entities.description: An advanced breeding line found to be resistant to dry root rot.

entities.name: BG 212
entities.description: A chickpea line used as a susceptible control for dry root rot.

entities.name: ICC 12267
entities.description: A chickpea line used as a susceptible control for dry root rot.
